pattern: Sorting lets a fixed first element plus a two-pointer sweep close in on the sum nearest the target in quadratic time.
hints: Once the array is sorted, fixing one number reduces the task to finding the closest pair sum to an adjusted target.
For each fixed index, walk a left and right pointer inward, moving whichever side brings the sum closer to the target.

Sort the array. For each i, set l=i+1, r=n-1; track the sum with the smallest absolute distance to target, and move l up if the current sum is below target or r down if above. Return the best sum found.

edge_inputs.description: two candidate sums equidistant on opposite sides of target
exact match exists and must win over a near miss
duplicates do not change the closest sum

stress.description: large array of random integers with a random target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(-1000, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randrange(-10000, 10001)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: at least three elements; values within constraints
